Fixes ShipLog not surviving a reboot, and makes empty Ollama summaries diagnosable.

## Fixed
- **Survives a reboot.** The engine was being started before the array/pools were
  mounted, so its SQLite version-memory landed on the root tmpfs and was wiped on
  every restart — which is why the remembered versions kept disappearing after a
  reboot. Creating `/mnt/user/...` that early can even stop the user share (shfs)
  from mounting. `rc.shiplog` now **defers the start until `DATA_DIR` is on a real
  filesystem** (not the root tmpfs); `event/started` brings the daemon up once the
  array is online, so the database persists across restarts.

## Changed
- **Ollama diagnostics.** When an AI summary comes back empty, the log now states
  the exact reason — HTTP status, an empty model response, or model output that
  isn't the expected JSON — instead of a silent "returned nothing", so summary
  problems can actually be tracked down.
